subject: Mathematics
title: Multiplicative Groups
description: This lecture explores the concept of multiplicative groups, which are groups formed under the operation of multiplication. We will focus on the multiplicative group of integers modulo n. This group consists of the integers that are coprime to n, with the group operation being multiplication modulo n. We will examine the structure of these groups, their properties, and how they relate to cyclic groups and Euler's totient function. By understanding multiplicative groups, we can gain insights into number theory and its applications in cryptography and other areas of mathematics.
objectives: Connect multiplicative groups to cyclic groups and Euler's totient function
prerequisites: Basic understanding of Euler's totient function
